description: Capture inbox-worthy content from sessions and prime sessions with the wiki index for recall

hooks.prompt:
Read ~/.claude/ziki.md using the Read tool. If the file does not exist, return {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": ""}} immediately — Ziki is not configured for this user.

If it exists, parse the YAML frontmatter for vault_owner, vault_repo, vault_branch and read the markdown body for vault access instructions. You MUST use the access method described in that file for all vault operations. Do NOT read from the local filesystem. Do NOT use ~/Documents/ziki/ or any local path.

Using that access method, fetch two files from the vault:

1. `wiki/_index.md` — the human-curated map of wiki pages
2. `_meta/taxonomy.md` — the tag vocabulary (optional, include if small)

Your job is to prime this Claude Code session with awareness of the Ziki wiki so it can retrieve pages on demand. Return a JSON object in this exact shape:

{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",
    "additionalContext": "<markdown block described below>"
  }
}

The `additionalContext` markdown block MUST contain:

- A one-paragraph preamble explaining that a Ziki wiki is available, where it lives (owner/repo/branch), and that the `ziki-recall` skill must be used to fetch pages when the current task touches an indexed topic. Tell the model not to answer from memory when a matching wiki page exists.
- The full contents of `wiki/_index.md` verbatim, inside a fenced block labeled `wiki/_index.md`.
- If fetched, an abbreviated taxonomy (tag list only, no descriptions) inside a fenced block labeled `_meta/taxonomy.md`.
- A short 'How to recall' section reminding the model to call the `ziki-recall` skill with the page path or title, and that the skill will follow [[wikilinks]] up to a small budget.

Size budget: keep `additionalContext` under 8KB. If `wiki/_index.md` is larger than 6KB, include only its top-level headings and first sentence of each entry, and note that the full index is available via `ziki-recall _index`.

If the vault read fails (auth, network, empty repo), return additionalContext with a one-line notice: `Ziki vault configured but unreachable — recall unavailable this session.` Do NOT fail the hook.

Read ~/.claude/ziki.md using the Read tool. If the file does not exist, return {"ok": true} immediately.

If it exists, parse the YAML frontmatter for vault_owner, vault_repo, vault_branch, and read the markdown body for access instructions. You MUST use the access method described in that file for all vault operations. Do NOT write to the local filesystem. Do NOT use ~/Documents/ziki/ or any local path.

The conversation context is about to be compacted. Identify anything worth preserving in the Ziki knowledge base before it is compressed: new concepts, URLs, technical findings, research, decisions.

For each item worth preserving, use the ziki-add skill. The skill will also read ~/.claude/ziki.md for access instructions.

Skip anything already filed, trivial content, or pure task execution.

When done, return {"ok": true}.
